text: In this work, we introduce a new framework referred to as Forward-Looking Active REtrieval augmented generation (FLARE). The common retrieve-and-generate approach of retrieval-augmented LMs only retrieves information based on the input query for one time, which is insufficient for long-form generation due to the evolving information needed for long texts. Currently, LMs have no mechanism to determine when and what information should be retrieved during generation. For this reason, we propose a model that can dynamically decide when and what to retrieve during the generation process in order to prevent hallucinations and maintain factual accuracy. The basic idea is to monitor the output probability distribution of the LM and treat any temporary generated sentence that contains rare tokens as a placeholder for future intention in the context of the input query. The model then treats the generated sentence as a query to search a document corpus for relevant documents, and generates the sentence again based on the searched knowledge. We experiment our proposed model with four knowledge-intensive tasks, including multi-hop reasoning, open-domain summarization, and long-form question answering, and show that FLARE significantly outperforms the baselines (i.e single-time and multi-time retrieval) for all tasks.
